_doc: ESB project contract for npamp_protocol, extended with Section F spec-parity gates. gates[] are the executable acceptance gates run by esb-gate.ps1 and in CI; a required failure fails the gate and writes honest evidence to .shippable/evidence/latest.json. The spec-parity gates make Class-B-without-impl a VISIBLE tracked gap, not a silent pass: they do not fail merely because a gap exists (gaps are legitimate and owned), they fail when the ledger is DISHONEST (a spec declares un-graded but has no entry; an entry claims graded/wired but its anchors do not resolve; a Class-B doc that gained an impl was never promoted).

gates.name: pins
gates.purpose: Vector/KAT bytes are provably the bytes shipped (NEP-0000 §6.2 pinning).

gates.name: schemas
gates.purpose: Every vector validates against its draft-2020-12 JSON Schema.

gates.name: go-reference-impl
gates.purpose: The primary reference implementation and its KAT tests pass (NEP-0000 §6a).

gates.name: conformance-corpus
gates.purpose: Class W + Class H machine-graded against the non-circular corpus/KATs (§55).

gates.name: spec-parity-coverage
gates.purpose: EVERY companion under spec/companion/ that declares an un-graded (Class B / specification-audited) posture has a matching, honest ledger entry (spec_anchor resolves, owner present, gate present). A companion doc with an un-graded self-label and NO ledger entry FAILS this gate -- that is the spec-without-implementation gap made visible. A tracked gap (impl_status planned, conformance_status spec-audited, owner+gate present) PASSES: the gate enforces tracking, not completion.

gates.name: spec-parity-honesty
gates.purpose: Every ledger entry claiming impl_status=wired has resolving impl_anchors, and every entry claiming conformance_status=graded has resolving vector_anchors that are pinned. A false-green (wired/graded claim whose anchors do not resolve) FAILS -- honesty over green (esb-wiring-gate semantics applied to spec parity).
